title: Intermittent Fasting Enhances Leptin Sensitivity via JMJD3-Mediated Epigenetic Regulation
authors_full: Smita Mall, Busayo Oladun, and Min-Hyun Kim
authors_short: Smita Mall Busayo Oladun & Min-Hyun Kim

talktrack_2m.what_this_is_about: This poster tests whether intermittent fasting (IF) can restore leptin sensitivity in diet-induced obese mice by increasing hypothalamic leptin receptor (LepRb)...
talktrack_2m.what_they_did: Animal experimental study (diet-induced obesity \+ dietary intervention \+ molecular assays \+ viral knockdown).. * Figure 1: n=8 per group
talktrack_2m.what_they_found: IF improves leptin sensitivity in obese mice without altering overall body weight or fat mass.
talktrack_2m.why_it_matters: IF may counter leptin resistance by changing hypothalamic gene regulation rather than by reducing body weight alone.
talktrack_2m.one_smart_follow_up: How did you define and operationalize “leptin sensitivity” in this study (food intake suppression, pSTAT3 response, or both)?

key_numbers: 7-week-old male C57BL/6J mice induced with obesity using high-fat diet (HFD).
Sample sizes ranged from n=4–8 per group depending on experiment; significance markers reported as p<0.05 and *p<0.01.
Male C57BL/6J mice were made obese on a high-fat diet, then assigned to ad libitum feeding or alternate-day fasting for...
takeaways: IF improves leptin sensitivity in obese mice without altering overall body weight or fat mass.
IF enhances hypothalamic LepRb expression and leptin signaling (increased STAT3 phosphorylation and target gene activation).
Epigenetic analysis shows reduced H3K27 methylation at the LepRb promoter, suggesting transcriptional activation under IF.
